# Business Case — Invoice-to-Pay Automation, Procurement Operations

## Current State

The accounts-payable team processes vendor invoices through a manual, three-touch workflow. The
baseline over FY25:

- Average invoice cycle time: 9.4 days[^ap-timestudy]
- Exception/rework rate: 18% of invoices require manual rework[^ap-finance]

## Proposed Change

Replace the manual entry-and-matching steps with OCR capture and an automated three-way match, routing
only true exceptions to a human.

## Impact

The annualized benefit is built from three components:

<!-- reconcile -->
- Labor savings (redeployed AP hours): 410,000
- Rework reduction savings: 95,000
- License consolidation savings: 120,000
- Total annual savings: 700,000
<!-- /reconcile -->

Automated matching cuts cycle time 30% and slashes the exception rate, making this the best-in-class
AP operation in the division.

## Risks

- **OCR accuracy on non-standard invoices:** scanned PDFs from small vendors may mis-capture.

## Sources

[^ap-timestudy]: AP time study, Procurement Operations, conducted 2025-11-10.
[^ap-finance]: FY2025 AP cost and exception report, Finance shared service, dated 2026-01-15.
